text: The integration of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in the Nigerian universities is a development that has been assimilated into the education sector with unprecedented speed. This unprecedented speed with which the technology has been adopted signals not only the enhanced teaching and learning that is expected to be achieved, but also a number of changes at the pedagogical, administrative levels and even a more personalized learning experience. But the use of this technology also introduces a whole gamut of ethical challenges that need to be addressed in order to effectively curtail the malicious use of this technology. This paper therefore focuses on the ethical issues that may emanate from the deployment of the technology with particular reference to data privacy, bias in the algorithms used for any applications, fairness in the dissemination of opportunities and the prevention of widening of the socio-economic inequalities inherent in the system as well as intellectual property rights and academic integrity. Using the extant regulations within our higher institutions and with reference to the inherent digital divide within our country, this paper posits the necessity for regulatory frameworks on the ethical uses of AI technology. The study reveals that the adoption of AI technologies holds great promise of improving education and also achieving the educational goals in Nigeria, but it must be treated with cultural and ethical considerations in order to protect the rights of students and teachers. This paper therefore recommended that all stakeholders, in order to build an ethical and sustainable AI technology, should develop a strategy on the use of technology that balances progress and justice.
